description:
# fz-PROV: Provenance-based spec lifetime in the typer

## The thing in one sentence

Replace the typer's "emit specs into a flat bag, prune at the end"
model with proper emitter→target provenance: each spec exists *because*
some currently-walking emitter site currently points to it, and the
end-of-typing sweep walks the actual emitted edges rather than
recomputing keys from final state.

## Why now

fz-5j5 (the previous typer epic) shipped a 10× speedup but left
structural smells behind. Honest list:

- The reachability sweep is a band-aid covering "we accumulated
  zombie cont specs whose slot-0 inputs shifted mid-flight."
- The MakeClosure-side any-key registration is still phase-separated
  inside a `loop { sweep; drain; ... }` block, despite the previous
  epic claiming the phase distinction collapsed.
- `compute_reachable_specs` duplicates key-computation logic from
  `walk_spec_for_discovery`. A real bug in that duplication
  (closure_lit slot-0 missing) only surfaced because TYP.3 ran them
  separately — under the old "rebuild everything from the same wrong
  logic each iter" pattern, the disagreement was invisible.

Every one of these falls out of a single missing invariant: we don't
track who emitted each spec.

## The structural invariant

Every spec in `specs` has ≥1 entry in `holders`. The end-of-typing
reachability pass walks the holders graph from `entry_seeds` — a real
graph traversal, not a re-derivation. Cycles without root-anchored
holders are unreachable and get collected.

```
EmitterSite = (caller_spec_key, block_id, EmitSlot)
EmitSlot    = Direct | Cont | ClosureLit(clause_idx, sig_idx)
            | MakeClosure(stmt_idx)

produces: HashMap<EmitterSite, SpecKey>
holders : HashMap<SpecKey, HashSet<EmitterSite>>
```

When a caller spec re-walks, each emitter's target may have changed.
The walk emits a complete `Vec<(EmitterSite, SpecKey)>`. The driver
diffs against `produces[E]` per site and transitions `produces` and
`holders` accordingly. No special-case "slot-0 change detection" —
just re-emit everything and let the diff sort it out.

## Concrete acceptance

- `compute_reachable_specs` deleted entirely (~125 lines).
- `sweep_makeclosure` and its outer loop deleted entirely (~70 lines).
- MakeClosure-side any-key registration flows through the same
  worklist as Direct/Cont/ClosureLit, gated on a live
  `opaque_arities: HashSet<usize>` with a `pending_makeclosure_by_arity`
  index for retroactive activation.
- No \`type_fn\` cache invariant violation: each spec key is type_fn'd
  at most once across an entire `type_module` call.
- Every test in the fixture matrix passes (interp + JIT + AOT) with
  no further golden re-blessing.
- A small basket of deterministic typer-work counters is pinned by
  test (PROV.4) and stays under its bounds.
- Termination proof documented in code; debug-build assertion catches
  any future violation.

## What this is NOT

- A rewrite of `Descr` operations or the set-theoretic core.
- A change to per-callsite specialization policy.
- A change to `type_fn` itself.
- An attempt to canonicalize Descr operations or change the
  effective_returns lattice.

## Removals are first-class

Each ticket explicitly enumerates what comes out. See PROV.6's body
for the full list.

## DAG

```
PROV.1  Backfill: extract closure_lit cont_key_for_spec fix from
        fz-5j5.3 into its own commit + add focused regression test
PROV.2  Investigate: why type_module fires 4× under \`fz run\`
PROV.3  Remove unnecessary caller_ft.clone() in process_worklist
PROV.4  Pin: basket of deterministic typer-work counters
PROV.5  Audit: stale comments referencing prev_returns / phase 1-2
                  │
                  ▼
PROV.6  Provenance refactor: EmitterSite + produces/holders;
        unified worklist; reachability via holders graph;
        MakeClosure folded in via live opaque_arities
                  │
                  ▼
PROV.7  Termination proof + debug-build counter-bound assertion
```

`.1` through `.5` are mutually independent and can land in any order.
`.4` should land before `.6` so the perf pin catches refactor
regressions. `.7` follows `.6`.

## Process discipline (not a ticket)

When a test fails after a refactor, BEFORE editing code, write down a
one-sentence answer to "is my output worse, equivalent, or better
than expected?" If you can't answer in one sentence, you don't
understand the failure yet. fz-5j5 nearly produced a workaround for
a real bug (closure_lit cont keying) because this discipline lapsed.

## Out of epic

- CLIF golden normalization (sorting/renaming u0:NN consistently
  before diff) — separate concern about test infrastructure, not
  about the typer's correctness or speed.
title: [EPIC] Typer: provenance-based spec lifetime
